title: rubus × coruscus
intro: Rubus × coruscus, also known as Japanese wineberry, is a hybrid species of raspberry native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of ellagic acid and anthocyanins. The health benefits of Rubus × coruscus include reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially aiding in cancer prevention. Rubus × coruscus can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional East Asian medicine as a treatment for various ailments, including fever and digestive issues.
